channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Utah governor signs collective bargaining ban for teachers, firefighters and police unions
item.description: Utah’s Republican governor has signed a collective bargaining ban that experts are calling one of the most restrictive labor laws in the country, despite overwhelming opposition from union members.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Schoenbaum, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sarah Palin ‘would love to’ join Trump administration
item.description: She pointed to "gatekeepers" within the administration, telling NewsNation: "It gets frustrating for those with a heart to serve.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's reciprocal tariffs will overturn decades of trade policy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is taking a blowtorch to the rules that have governed world trade for decades. The “reciprocal’’ tariffs that he announced Thursday are likely to create chaos for global businesses and conflict with America’s allies and adversaries alike.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN and CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The political fallout from former Supervisor Zack Scrivner's resignation, investigation into alleged abuse
item.description: Scrivner resigned from his county supervisor's seat in August 2024. Potential candidates said the timing of the move hindered their campaigns to fill a seat.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Huh, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Scrivner’s legal woes shattered family political network
item.description: The saga surrounding Zack Scrivner isn't just a story about his alleged crimes. He was one of the most well-connected politicians in Kern County history.
item.dc:creator: Robert Price, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Winners and losers: Who came out on top
item.description: There were only so many chair positions to go around, with 30 committees for 88 Republicans, making it inevitable that some would miss out on plum appointments.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By James Barragán And Jasper Scherer, KPRC 2

item.title: Alleged DOGE employees show up at San Francisco City Hall
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- An official with the San Francisco Sheriff’s Office confirmed to NewsNation affiliate KRON4 that it is investigating a report of three individuals claiming to be Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) employees who attempted to enter offices in San Francisco City Hall on
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Judge extends order blocking Musk’s DOGE team from Treasury payment system
item.description: A federal judge in New York on Friday extended a temporary order blocking the Elon Musk-run Department of Government Efficiency from accessing Treasury Department payment systems.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department moves to drop case against NYC Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: Following the departure of several federal prosecutors, the Department of Justice moved to dismiss corruption charges against the Big Apple's mayor.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Trump official teases 2026 bid for California governor if Harris jumps in race
item.description: Richard Grenell, a longtime Donald Trump loyalist who is serving as U.S. envoy for special missions in the president's second administration, is floating a potential bid for California governor if former Vice President Kamala Harris also runs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dems likely to 'waste millions' on deluge of lawsuits but could cost Trump precious time: expert
item.description: Democrats will likely 'waste millions' trying to litigate Trump's executive orders and lose, University of California, Berkeley, law professor John Yoo told Fox News Digital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Treasury watchdog begins audit of Musk DOGE team's access to the US government's payment system
item.description: The Treasury Department’s Office of Inspector General says it's launching an audit of the security controls for the federal government’s payment system.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein And Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 83 percent say president is required to follow Supreme Court rulings: Survey
item.description: Just over half, 51 percent, of Americans approve of the job the Supreme Court is doing, which is the highest amount of support it’s received in the last three years.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge sets a 5-day deadline for the Trump administration to start lifting its USAID funding freeze
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge has ordered President Donald Trump's administration to temporarily lift a funding freeze that has shut down U.S. humanitarian aid and development work around the world, and he has set a five-day deadline for the administration to prove it's complying.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Is Elon Musk overreaching with DOGE? Lawmakers debate
item.description: Is Elon Musk, head of DOGE, being transparent? Reps. Ro Khanna, D-Calif., and Byron Donalds, R-Fla., debate on NewsNation's "CUOMO.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Hungary's Orbán predicts Trump will bring Russia back into Western fold after end of Ukraine war
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump's administration will reconnect Russia with the economies and energy networks of Western countries if the war in Ukraine, now nearly three years old, comes to an end, Hungary's nationalist prime minister said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Including people without legal status in census has had little impact on House seats, study finds
item.description: Republicans are trying again to exclude people who are in the U.S. illegally from the numbers used to portion out congressional seats among the states. But a new study says their inclusion in the past four censuses has had little impact on presidential elections or control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Plane carrying Secretary of State Rubio to Europe turned around because of a mechanical issue
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Air Force plane carrying Secretary of State Marco Rubio and the Senate Foreign Relations Committee chairman, Sen. Jim Risch, to Germany for the Munich Security Conference was forced to return to Washington late Thursday after developing a mechanical issue.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OPM directs agencies to fire government workers still on probation
item.description: Office of Personnel Management (OPM) officials met with agency leaders across government Thursday and directed them to begin firing employees still in their probationary period a year or more after being hired.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Suspended Hillsborough Democrat defends online posts some say are antisemitic
item.description: The Hillsborough County Democratic Executive Committee has suspended a member until January 2027 over posts online and wording in email messages.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republicans advance anti-abortion legislation during control of Minnesota House
item.description: Republicans have control of the Minnesota House at least through a special election in March, giving them a chance to advance abortion legislation that would have a tougher path in a tied chamber.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Legislature OKs bill targeting high egg prices, allowing suspension of cage-free requirement
item.description: Nevada lawmakers on Wednesday gave final approval to a bill that could bring down the price of eggs, clearing the way for Gov. Joe Lombardo to give it his signature.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump funding freeze could leave communities on their own as climate threats grow
item.description: Without federal support, American communities will struggle to deal with a challenge as pervasive as climate change, market analysts and environmental advocates say.
item.dc:creator: Michael Copley, MPR News

item.title: Gov. Hochul reacts to pending dismissal of corruption case against Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The pending dismissal of the corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams has partners in running the city and political opponents raising concerns about whether Adams can run the city. Gov. Kathy Hochul even said she does not know if the mayor is “compromised.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Musk defends DOGE alongside Trump in striking Oval Office moment
item.description: Elon Musk on Tuesday defended the work of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) alongside President Trump, who invited Musk to speak and sat at the resolute desk as the tech mogul gave a lengthy address.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon pleads guilty and avoids jail time in border wall fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon pleaded guilty on Tuesday to defrauding donors to a private effort to build a wall on the U.S. southern border, ending a case the conservative strategist decried as a “political persecution.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pope criticizes Trump administration's immigration policies
item.description: Pope Francis criticized President Donald Trump’s mass deportation plans in a letter urging compassion and the preservation of dignity for migrants in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Florida lawmakers to begin third special session on illegal immigration
item.description: After a particularly contentious few weeks, Florida lawmakers will be heading back to Tallahassee Tuesday for the purpose of passing legislation on illegal immigration — this time with the governor's support.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump fires Office of Government Ethics chief
item.description: President Trump has fired the director of the Office of Government Ethics, the agency announced Monday. The office posted on its website that it had been notified Trump was removing David Huitema, who had been nominated by former President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump tells Treasury Department to stop making pennies
item.description: President Trump claims he has instructed the U.S. Treasury Department to stop producing pennies, calling the 1-cent coin wasteful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Senate Democrats launch portal for whistleblowers in Trump administration
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and Sen. Gary Peters (D-Mich.), the ranking member of the Senate Homeland Security Committee, said they are launching a new portal for whistleblowers to expose wrongdoing and abuses of power in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5
